# ThriveStack Product Telemetry — AI Instrumentation Guide (B2C)
# Version: 3.2
# Hosted at: https://docs.thrivestack.ai/ai-setup/product/b2c/llms.txt

---

## PURPOSE

This file gives AI coding assistants complete, executable instructions for instrumenting
any existing B2C web app with ThriveStack product telemetry.
Every rule, schema, and example here is authoritative — follow exactly, do not deviate.

This guide is for **B2C applications** — each user is their own independent account with no org or workspace entity.

All event schemas are non-exhaustive. Additional properties beyond those listed are always allowed.

---

## HOW TO USE THIS GUIDE

Work through these phases in order. Do not write any code until Phase 0 is complete.

### Phase 0 — Understand the codebase first
0. Determine if the codebase is **frontend/full-stack** or **backend-only**:
   - If it runs in a browser or has a frontend entry point (Next.js, React, Vue, Nuxt, Angular, Svelte, plain HTML), continue with this guide.
   - If it is a **backend/server-side application with no browser context** (Node.js API server, Express, Fastify, Python/Django/FastAPI/Flask, Go service, Ruby on Rails API, etc.), **stop here** and use the backend guide instead:
     → https://docs.thrivestack.ai/ai-setup/product/backend/llms.txt
1. This is a **B2C** app — each user is their own independent account. Do NOT search for org/workspace/account entities. Use the B2C pattern for `group()` (STEP 4).
2. Identify the tech stack, auth library, and state management
3. Find `userId` — see STEP 2 for search patterns and rules
4. Find all instrumentable feature actions. Then **STOP** and ask the developer, exactly:
   > "I found these features: [list each one]. For each, which type applies?
   > - **core** — primary value driver, reason users signed up (e.g. Report builder, Pipeline view)
   > - **sticky** — habit-forming, pulls users back daily/weekly (e.g. Notifications, Workflow automation)
   > - **support** — switching cost builder, embeds product in operations (e.g. SSO, Billing, Audit logs)"
   Do not write any `feature_used` calls until the developer has answered for every feature.
5. **STOP** and ask the developer:
   > "Do you want to check for abusive or disposable email addresses during signup?
   > - **(a) Block** — prevent the signup and show an error message
   > - **(b) Flag** — allow signup but mark the user for manual review
   > - **(c) Skip** — do not implement abuse checking"
   Do not write any `checkAbuseEmail` call until the developer has answered. If they choose (c), omit the abuse check entirely and mark row 11 as N/A in the summary.

### Phase 1 — Install the script tag
Add once to the root layout. Follow STEP 1 exactly for the detected framework.

### Phase 2 — Instrument lifecycle events
Follow the FIRING ORDER section exactly for each flow found: signup, login.

### Phase 3 — Instrument feature events
One `feature_used` event per confirmed feature action. `feature_name` in `[noun]_[verb]` snake_case.

### Phase 4 — Verify then summarise
Run through the VERIFICATION CHECKLIST. Fix anything missing. Then output the summary.
After the summary is printed, your task is complete. Do not continue. Do not fix unrelated issues, refactor surrounding code, or make any further edits.

**REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT — strictly follow this, no exceptions.**
Do NOT write a narrative summary. Do NOT output a "Summary of Changes" section. Do NOT describe what you did in prose.
Your only output after instrumentation is complete is the table below, printed inline in the chat. No files. No markdown files. No prose before or after it.

```
ThriveStack Instrumentation Summary
X/12 implemented

| # | Event | Status | Notes |
|---|-------|--------|-------|
| 0 | script tag URL | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Exact src used |
| 1 | identify | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Field used, file |
| 2 | group | ✅/⚠️/❌ | B2C fallback — group_id = user.id |
| 3 | signed_up | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file |
| 4 | signed_in | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file |
| 5 | account_created | N/A | B2C — no org account created |
| 6 | account_added_user | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Entry points covered |
| 7 | feature_used | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Features and their feature_type |
| 8 | invite_sent | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file or N/A |
| 9 | onboarding_step_completed | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Steps found or N/A |
| 10 | signed_out | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file |
| 11 | abuse_check | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Signup file, block / flag / N/A |

User ID: [field] in [file]
Group hierarchy: B2C fallback — group_id = user.id
Unresolved: [anything skipped and why, or "None"]

Instrumentation complete. Test your app as a real user — events will start flowing into ThriveStack.

Enabled Features:
- Departmental Analytics — X reports unlocked — insights across acquisition, activation, retention, and more.
  https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/home

---
Handy ThriveStack Resources
- Setup Progress: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/onboarding/product-analytics/telemetry
- Diagnostics: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/diagnostics-report
- API / Audit Logs: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/events
- Developer Tools: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/developer-tool
```

---

## STEP 1 — INSTALL THE SCRIPT TAG

This is the ONLY initialization method. There is no npm package. There is no init() call.

Add once to the root layout file. Never inside a component or page.

### Next.js App Router (app/layout.tsx)
```tsx
import Script from 'next/script'
export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    &lt;html&gt;
      &lt;head&gt;
        &lt;Script
          src="https://cdn.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/script.js"
          strategy="afterInteractive"
          api-key={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_THRIVESTACK_API_KEY}
          data-source="product"
        /&gt;
      &lt;/head&gt;
      &lt;body&gt;{children}&lt;/body&gt;
    &lt;/html&gt;
  )
}
```

### React (public/index.html)
```html
&lt;script
  src="https://cdn.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/script.js"
  api-key="%REACT_APP_THRIVESTACK_API_KEY%"
  source="product"&gt;
&lt;/script&gt;
```

### Vue / Nuxt / Plain HTML
```html
&lt;script
  src="https://cdn.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/script.js"
  api-key="YOUR_ENV_VAR_HERE"
  source="product"&gt;
&lt;/script&gt;
```

Rules:
- Write the API key to the environment variable file appropriate for this stack (.env, .env.local, application.yml, etc.) — never in source code
- `api-key` must reference that environment variable — never hardcoded
- `source="product"` — always this exact string, never change it
- Global object after load: `thriveStack` — camelCase, lowercase 's'
- Place in root layout ONCE only

---

## STEP 2 — FIND userId

Must be a stable unique identifier that does not change across sessions.
May be a UUID, integer, or any format the auth provider uses.
Prefer a stable unique ID over email. Email is acceptable as `user_id` if it is the only stable identifier available — use it without blocking.

Search patterns:
```javascript
const { user } = useAuth()               → user.id
const session = useSession()             → session.user.id
useContext(AuthContext)                  → context.user.id
const { userId } = auth()               → userId  (Clerk)
store.auth.user.id                       → (Redux/Zustand)
claims.sub, claims.user_id              → (JWT)
```

If no stable identifier found — stop and ask:
"What field uniquely identifies a user in your database — their ID, not their email?"

**Do NOT search for org, account, workspace, or tenant IDs.** This is a B2C app — the user IS their own group. See STEP 4 for how group() is called.

---

## STEP 3 — identify()

Call on signup, login, and whenever new/updated user info is gathered.
`user_id` is always mandatory. Send whatever is known at the time.

```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.identify([{
  "user_id": userId,
  "traits": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name
  },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

## STEP 4 — group() — B2C Pattern

In B2C, the user is their own account. Fire `group()` ONCE — only on first registration, never again.

```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.group([{
  "user_id": userId,
  "group_id": user.id,              // same as userId
  "traits": {
    "group_type": "Account",
    "account_name": user.name,      // user's full name
    "account_domain": user.email    // full email address — not just domain
    // omit plan_id and plan_name entirely
  },
  "context": {
    "group_id": user.id,            // must match group_id exactly
    "group_type": "Account"
  },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

Rules:
- Fire ONCE on first registration only — never on login, never on subsequent visits
- `group_id` = `user.id` (the same value as userId)
- `account_domain` = full email address (e.g. `"alice@gmail.com"`) — not just the domain
- Do NOT include `plan_id` or `plan_name`
- Do NOT make additional group() calls for other entity levels — there are none in B2C

---

## STEP 5 — TRACK EVENTS

### Base signature
```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "event_name_here",
  "user_id": userId,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name
  },
  "context": { "group_id": user.id },   // always user.id in B2C
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

## EVENT SCHEMAS

### signed_up
When: New user registration completes.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "signed_up",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "utm_source": getUTMParam('utm_source'),
    "utm_medium": getUTMParam('utm_medium'),
    "utm_campaign": getUTMParam('utm_campaign'),
    "utm_term": getUTMParam('utm_term')
  },
  "context": { "group_id": user.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])

function getUTMParam(param) {
  return new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).get(param)
    || sessionStorage.getItem(param)
    || null
}
```

---

### signed_in
When: Existing user logs in successfully.
No UTM params. `context` is mandatory.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "signed_in",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name
  },
  "context": { "group_id": user.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### signed_out
When: Existing user logs out.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "signed_out",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name
  },
  "context": { "group_id": user.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### account_created
**N/A for B2C.** Do not implement this event. There is no org/account created in a B2C flow.

---

### account_added_user
When: User completes registration (they are added to their own "account").
Fire once on signup only.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "account_added_user",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "account_id": user.id,
    "account_name": user.name,
    "account_domain": user.email,
    "join_method": "signup",
    "is_first_user": true
  },
  "context": { "group_id": user.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### onboarding_step_completed
When: User completes a discrete setup step.
Search for: `<Stepper>` `onStepComplete` `handleNext` `setCurrentStep` `markStepComplete`

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "onboarding_step_completed",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "step_name": stepName,      // snake_case: "connected_data_source"
    "step_index": stepIndex,    // 0-based integer
    "total_steps": totalSteps
  },
  "context": { "group_id": user.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### invite_sent
When: User sends an invitation (referral, share, team invite if any).
All properties beyond `user_email` and `invitee_email` are send-if-available.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "invite_sent",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "invitee_email": invitee.email,
    "source_url": window.location.href
  },
  "context": { "group_id": user.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### feature_used
When: User performs any core product action.

- `event_name` is ALWAYS the literal string `"feature_used"` — never a custom verb
- `feature_name` is always `[noun]_[verb]` snake_case
- `feature_type` must be confirmed with developer before instrumenting — never assume

Ask the developer which applies:
- `"core"` — primary value driver, reason users signed up (e.g. Report builder, Pipeline view)
- `"sticky"` — habit-forming, pulls users back daily/weekly (e.g. Notifications, Workflow automation)
- `"support"` — switching cost builder, embeds product in operations (e.g. SSO, Billing, Audit logs)

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "feature_used",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "feature_name": "export_report",
    "feature_type": "core"
  },
  "context": { "group_id": user.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### checkAbuseEmail — Abuse Prevention
When: Before signup form submission completes.
Prerequisites: ThriveStack SDK script tag installed (STEP 1).

Use the answer from Phase 0 step 5 (block / flag / skip). If skip was chosen, omit this section entirely.

```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
const checkAbuse = await thriveStack.checkAbuseEmail(userEmail)
if (checkAbuse.success && checkAbuse.data.isAbuse === 1) {
  // BLOCK path — prevent form submission and show error
  showError(checkAbuse.message)

  // FLAG path — allow signup but mark for review (use instead of block above)
  // flagUserForReview(userEmail, checkAbuse.data.reasonForAbuse)
}
```

Note: `await` is intentional here — this is a data check that gates the flow, not a fire-and-forget event.

`reasonForAbuse` fields: `isDisposable` (throwaway email provider), `domainExists` (false = domain not found), `isDuplicate` (email already seen in system).

---

## FIRING ORDER

### Signup (new user)
```
identify → group(user.id) → signed_up → account_added_user
```

### Login
```
identify → signed_in
```

### Logout
```
signed_out
```

---

## VERIFICATION CHECKLIST

Before delivering the summary, verify all 12 points. Fix anything missing first.

- [ ] 0. script tag — src is exactly `https://cdn.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/script.js` — no variation, no substitution. Known wrong URLs to reject: any `cloudfront.net` URL, any `/v1/te.js` path, any URL that is not this exact string
- [ ] 1. identify — fires on signup, login, profile/info updates
- [ ] 2. group — fires ONCE on first registration only, using B2C pattern (group_id = user.id)
- [ ] 3. signed_up — correct flow, UTM captured
- [ ] 4. signed_in — fires on every login, context block present
- [ ] 5. account_created — mark N/A (B2C — no org account)
- [ ] 6. account_added_user — fires on signup
- [ ] 7. feature_used — at least one, feature_type confirmed with developer
- [ ] 8. invite_sent — all invite handlers found, or N/A if no invite flow exists
- [ ] 9. onboarding_step_completed — all stepper/wizard flows found, or N/A
- [ ] 10. signed_out — fires on logout, context block present
- [ ] 11. abuse_check — if chosen in Phase 0: checkAbuseEmail called before signup submission, block or flag implemented; if skipped: mark N/A

### Summary format

Output this summary directly in the chat as your final message. Do not create any files for this summary. Do not write it to markdown. Print it inline.

**ThriveStack Instrumentation Summary**
X/12 implemented

| # | Event | Status | Notes |
|---|-------|--------|-------|
| 0 | script tag URL | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Exact src used |
| 1 | identify | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Field used, file |
| 2 | group | ✅/⚠️/❌ | B2C fallback — group_id = user.id |
| 3 | signed_up | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file |
| 4 | signed_in | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file |
| 5 | account_created | N/A | B2C — no org account created |
| 6 | account_added_user | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Entry points covered |
| 7 | feature_used | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Features and their feature_type |
| 8 | invite_sent | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file or N/A |
| 9 | onboarding_step_completed | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Steps found or N/A |
| 10 | signed_out | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file |
| 11 | abuse_check | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Signup file, block / flag / N/A |

**User ID:** [field] in [file]
**Group hierarchy:** B2C fallback — group_id = user.id
**Unresolved:** [anything skipped and why, or "None"]

Instrumentation complete. Test your app as a real user — events will start flowing into ThriveStack.

**Enabled Features:**
- Departmental Analytics — X reports unlocked — insights across acquisition, activation, retention, and more.
  https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/home

---

**Handy ThriveStack Resources**
- Setup Progress: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/onboarding/product-analytics/telemetry
- Diagnostics: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/diagnostics-report
- API / Audit Logs: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/events
- Developer Tools: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/developer-tool

---

## HARD RULES

1. Script src must be exactly `https://cdn.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/script.js` — never alter the URL. Known wrong substitutions to reject: any `cloudfront.net` URL, any `/v1/te.js` path, any URL that is not this exact string
2. Global object: `thriveStack` — never `ThriveStack` or `thrivestack`
3. Every method takes an array: `identify([{...}])` `group([{...}])` `track([{...}])`
4. Guard every call: `if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return`
5. timestamp: `new Date().toISOString()` on every call — when multiple events fire in the same handler, use `const now = Date.now()` once and offset each by 1ms: `new Date(now).toISOString()`, `new Date(now + 1).toISOString()`, etc.
6. `group_type: "Account"` — used in B2C with user.id as group_id
7. `context.group_id` must equal `group_id` on group() — always `user.id` in B2C
8. `context` required on every `track()` call — use `user.id` as `group_id`
9. userId from auth context only — never hardcode, never generate
10. `api-key` from environment variable only — never in source code
11. Fire-and-forget — never `await` thriveStack calls
12. Fire after action succeeds — inside `.then()` or after `await`
13. `group()` fires ONCE on first registration only — never on login or any subsequent visit
14. `feature_used` is always the literal event_name — never a custom verb
15. `feature_type` confirmed with developer — never assumed. Never write a `feature_used` call without an explicit answer from the developer for that feature's type. Skipping this question is not allowed.
15a. Abuse check question is mandatory — never skip it. Never implement `checkAbuseEmail` without first asking the developer whether they want block, flag, or to skip entirely. Never assume any option.
16. `account_added_user` fires on signup — the user is added to their own "account"
17. Do NOT implement `account_created` — it is N/A for B2C
18. Additional properties beyond documented ones are always permitted
19. Stop after the summary is output — do not fix bugs, refactor code, clean up existing logic, or make any other changes outside of ThriveStack instrumentation. Your only permitted modifications are the ThriveStack calls added in this guide.
20. Never remove the ThriveStack script tag — once added, it must remain in `<head>` permanently. Any subsequent edit (OG meta tags, favicon, title, publishing steps, or any other change) must preserve it. After every edit, verify the script tag is still present; if it was removed, restore it immediately before continuing.

---

## CRITICAL MISTAKES

WRONG — searching for org/account IDs:
```javascript
// Do NOT do this in B2C
const groupId = user.orgId || user.accountId
```
RIGHT — use user.id directly:
```javascript
const groupId = user.id
```

WRONG — firing group() on login:
```javascript
async function handleLogin() {
  thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": user.id }])
}
```
RIGHT — group() only on first registration:
```javascript
async function handleSignup() {
  thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": user.id, ... }])
}
```

WRONG — implementing account_created:
```javascript
thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "account_created" }])
```
RIGHT — this event is N/A for B2C, do not implement it.

WRONG — missing array:
```javascript
thriveStack.track({ "event_name": "signed_up" })
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "signed_up" }])
```

WRONG — no guard:
```javascript
thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": user.id }])
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": user.id }])
```

WRONG — custom event_name for feature:
```javascript
thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "report_exported" }])
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "feature_used",
  "properties": { "feature_name": "report_exported", "feature_type": "core" }
}])
```
